name: Pan De Muertos (All Saints and All Souls Day Bread)
recipeIngredient: 8 egg yolk and 2 tbsp water, lightly beaten
1 tsp orange flower water or grated rind of 1 orange
recipeInstructions:
For the Starter:
1. Combine flour, salt, sugar, and yeast in a mixing bowl.
2. Gradually beat in water and eggs until cohesive mass forms around dough hook.
3. Dough should be sticky, elastic, and shiny; about 5 minutes.
4. Turn out onto floured board and form into round cushion shape.
5. Grease and flour a clean bowl, place dough inside, cover with greased plastic wrap and towel.
6. Let rise in warm place (ideally 70°F) until doubled in volume, about 2 hours.
For the Final Dough:
1. Grease four baking sheets for both breads.
2. Combine starter, sugar, and butter in a mixing bowl; mix well.
3. Gradually beat in flour and egg yolks alternately.
4. Beat in water and flavoring until slightly sticky, smooth, shiny dough that holds shape.
5. Form into round cushion shape on lightly floured surface.
6. Place dough in washed mixing bowl, cover with greased plastic wrap and towel.
7. Let rise in warm place (ideally about 70°F) for 1 1/2 hours until almost doubled in size, or overnight in refrigerator.
8. Bring dough to room temperature before working with it.
9. Divide into two equal pieces; set one aside.
10. Roll three-quarters of the dough into smooth ball, press out to circle about 8 inches in diameter and about 1 inch thick.
11. Form narrow ridge around edge like hat brim; transfer to greased baking sheet. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let rise half its size again for 1 hour.
12. Divide remaining quarter of the dough into four equal parts, roll one into smooth ball.
13. Roll other three into strips about 8 inches long, with knobs at each end and two in middle.
14. Transfer to another greased tray; cover loosely with plastic wrap and let rise for 1 hour.
15. Repeat steps to form second bread with remaining dough.
16. Preheat oven to 375°F.
17. Carefully place strips across main part of the bread, place round ball in center to form skull.
18. Press fingers hard to form eye sockets; brush surface well with beaten yolks.
19. Bake until browned and springy, about 15 minutes.
20. Turn off oven, open door, let sit for 5 more minutes.
21. Remove from oven, brush with melted butter, and sprinkle generously with sugar.
